preamble: The voiced retroflex flap [ɽ] shifts to the alveolar lateral approximant [l] through deretroflexion and manner change. This transformation is documented in historical sound mergers and contemporary allophonic variation across Australian Aboriginal languages and some Indo-Aryan dialects.

examples.note: Contemporary phonetic variation; retroflex flap and lateral approximant in contrast
Casual/rapid speech allophone in intervocalic contexts

related.label: Deretroflexion to trill instead of lateral
